features.goal:
IVX HOLDINGS — BUILD THE INTERNAL ENGINEERING OPERATING SYSTEM

OBJECTIVE

Create an owner-controlled IVX Core Platform and autonomous App Factory that reduce future application development from months or years to weeks through reusable, verified infrastructure.

Do not create disconnected applications or duplicate systems.

============================================================
PHASE 1 — IVX CORE PLATFORM
============================================================

Create reusable production modules for:

1. Owner authentication and RBAC
2. Members and profiles
3. Investors and buyers
4. Organizations and teams
5. Properties, deals and investments
6. Chat and real-time messaging
7. Notifications
8. Image, video and document uploads
9. Reels and social engagement
10. Search and recommendations
11. Wallets and transaction ledger
12. Payments and reconciliation
13. Analytics and reporting
14. Audit and Developer Proof Ledger
15. Feature flags and configuration
16. Monitoring, alerts and recovery
17. Security and RLS policy templates
18. Backups and rollback

Every module must have:

- documented API
- database schema
- automated tests
- security tests
- performance tests
- version number
- owner approval state
- reusable installation process

============================================================
PHASE 2 — IVX APP FACTORY
============================================================

Create a controlled application-development pipeline:

IDEA
→ REQUIREMENTS
→ ARCHITECTURE
→ DESIGN
→ CODE
→ TEST
→ SECURITY
→ PREVIEW
→ OWNER APPROVAL
→ RELEASE
→ MONITORING

The factory must generate:

- Expo React Native Android/iOS/Web applications
- backend APIs
- database migrations
- automated tests
- deployment configuration
- health endpoints
- monitoring dashboards
- rollback packages
- complete documentation

============================================================
PHASE 3 — AI WORKFORCE
============================================================

Organize FA-01 through FA-50 into controlled teams.

Each agent must have:

- unique ID
- defined role
- allowed tools
- prohibited actions
- assigned workflow
- test task
- proof ledger
- performance score
- verified or unverified status

No agent may deploy directly except the Release Manager AI.

No agent is VERIFIED until it completes real work successfully.

============================================================
PHASE 4 — INSTAGRAM-LEVEL MEDIA SYSTEM
============================================================

Build and verify reusable modules for:

- reels feed
- media upload and processing
- image carousel
- likes
- comments
- saves
- shares
- follows
- notifications
- infinite scrolling
- caching
- background uploads
- playback recovery
- content moderation
- engagement analytics

Verify on Android, iOS and Web.

============================================================
PHASE 5 — ENTERPRISE COMMERCE SYSTEM
============================================================

Build reusable modules for:

- investor marketplace
- buyer marketplace
- catalog and search
- recommendations
- transactions
- wallet and statements
- payment reconciliation
- commissions
- customer support
- fraud and risk controls
- business analytics

============================================================
PHASE 6 — PILOT APPLICATION
============================================================

Use the factory to build one real pilot application from scratch.

Measure:

- total build time
- human interventions
- defects
- automated tests
- security findings
- deployment success
- rollback success
- operating cost

The pilot is the proof that the factory works.

============================================================
FINAL EVIDENCE
============================================================

Return:

- architecture map
- repositories
- module registry
- agent registry
- verified agent count
- reusable module count
- test results
- GitHub SHAs
- deployment IDs
- production health
- pilot application URL
- APK/AAB status
- remaining blockers
- estimated development-time reduction

Do not claim Amazon-level or Instagram-level readiness without performance, security, real-device and production evidence.

features.title: Apply this EXACT two-file production hardening patch to the AI runtime (emerg...
features.goal:
Apply this EXACT two-file production hardening patch to the AI runtime (emergency gateway repair, 2026-07-18). Do not change any other files. Do not redesign — apply precisely these edits:

FILE 1: backend/services/ivx-provider-state-machine.ts
1. Add module-level state after the `state` const declarations:
   let failureLatchedAtMs: number | null = null;
   const RECOVERY_COOLDOWN_MS = parsed from env IVX_AI_PROVIDER_RECOVERY_COOLDOWN_MS, default 60000 (validate finite > 0).
2. In markProviderFailed(): set failureLatchedAtMs = Date.now() when latching PROVIDER_FAILED.
3. In markAIUnavailable(): set failureLatchedAtMs = Date.now() when latching AI_UNAVAILABLE.
4. In markProviderReady() and markFallbackReady(): set failureLatchedAtMs = null.
5. In resetProviderStateMachine(): set failureLatchedAtMs = null.
6. Rewrite shouldTryPrimary() to: return true when state is not PROVIDER_FAILED and not AI_UNAVAILABLE; return false when state.credentialLoaded is false; if failureLatchedAtMs !== null and Date.now() - failureLatchedAtMs >= RECOVERY_COOLDOWN_MS then set state.state = 'PROVIDER_VALIDATING', set failureLatchedAtMs = null, log a recovery message, and return true; otherwise return false. This converts the permanent AI_UNAVAILABLE latch (root cause of the 2026-07-18 outage) into a half-open circuit breaker with cooldown recovery.

FILE 2: backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts
1. Change the first import from `import { generateText, streamText, createGateway } from 'ai';` to `import { generateText, streamText } from 'ai';` (remove createGateway - Vercel AI Gateway SDK dependency removed).
2. In requestIVXAIText primary attempt block: replace `const gatewayProvider = isVercelKey ? createGateway({ apiKey }) : createOpenAI({ apiKey, baseURL });` with `const gatewayProvider = createOpenAI({ apiKey, baseURL });` — the direct OpenAI-compatible client is used for BOTH key types with the key passed explicitly (baseURL already auto-selects https://api.openai.com/v1 for sk- keys and https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1 for vck_ keys). Keep isVercelKey only for the markProviderReady provider-name argument.
3. Wrap the primary attempt in a bounded retry loop: const MAX_PRIMARY_ATTEMPTS = 3; retry ONLY when classifyProviderFailure(error) is one of 'network' | 'server_error' | 'rate_limit' AND the failure is not auth (401/403); between attempts await an exponential backoff sleep of 600ms * 2^(attempt-1). Never retry auth failures — keep the existing markProviderFailed call for them and break.
4. In streamIVXAIText: replace the same `isVercelKey ? createGateway({ apiKey }) : createOpenAI({ apiKey, baseURL })` conditional with `const gatewayProvider = createOpenAI({ apiKey, baseURL });`.
5. In ensureIVXAIGatewayEnvironment(): remove the env-var bridging block that sets process.env.AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY (keep the missing-key throw).
6. Add in-flight idempotency deduplication: a module-level `const inFlightAIRequests = new Map<string, Promise<IVXAITextResult>>();` and make requestIVXAIText dedupe by key `${module}::${requestId}` when requestId is present — concurrent duplicates return the same promise; the map entry is deleted when the promise settles.

VALIDATION: run typecheck; the existing backend/tests/ivx-provider-state-machine.test.ts must still pass unchanged (cooldown default 60s means immediate shouldTryPrimary checks after failure still return false).
